---
title: "LRP67: What within-child changes predict reading change? (latent change-score model)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP67 is the **within-child, longitudinal** complement to LRP65. LRP65 asks which
baseline skills predict a child's overall word-reading *gain* (a between-child
contrast). LRP67 unrolls that question over all four waves with a **latent
change-score model (LCSM)**: for each wave-to-wave transition, does a child's
prior-wave **letter-sound** and **vocabulary** standing predict their subsequent
**reading** change?

This is "Approach 4" (process-over-time) from the modelling meeting, and one
longitudinal triangulation point beside the between-child LRP65 model. The
RI-CLPM companion was dropped as not estimable at this sample size.

::: {.callout-note title="Symbol mapping (read before the table)"}
The codebase `MEASURES` symbols differ from the handoff's R / L / V:

| Construct | Symbol here | Measure | Denominator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Reading | **W** | `ewrswr` | 79 |
| Letter-sounds | **L** | `yarclet` | 32 |
| Expressive vocabulary | **E** | `eowpvt` | 170 |

`R` is **not** reading here — it is receptive vocabulary (`rowpvt`). The handoff's
headline path "L → R over time" is the coefficient `g_L` (prior letter-sounds →
reading change) below.
:::

## Model structure

For each measure $m \in \{W, L, E\}$, child $i$ and wave $t$, a latent logit
true-score $x_m[i,t]$ accumulates change scores (a McArdle latent change-score
model with **process noise**):

$$
x_m[i,t] = x_m[i,t-1] + \Delta_m[i,t],
\qquad
\Delta_m[i,t] = \overline{\Delta}_m[i,t] + \sigma^{\text{proc}}_m \, z_m[i,t].
$$

The **reading** change is coupled to prior-wave levels — the headline:

$$
\overline{\Delta}_W = a_W + b_W\,x_W[t-1] + g_L\,x_L[t-1] + g_E\,x_E[t-1]
                    + d^{\text{age}}_W\,\text{age}[t-1].
$$

Letter-sounds and vocabulary changes follow a self-proportional form plus age. The
intervention-dose covariate is **omitted** — it is the revised DAG's `IS` collider, so
conditioning on it would reopen the latent-`GA` backdoor onto the `g_L`/`g_E` couplings
(ID-3). Coupling coefficients are **time-invariant** (pooled across the three
transitions) — a deliberate constraint at this sample size. Observed counts enter a
**masked** Beta-Binomial so a child missing one score still contributes its other
waves; $\kappa$ is measurement overdispersion, distinct from the dynamic
$\sigma^{\text{proc}}$.
{{< include _partials/_results_lcsm.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
